inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** â€” Symlink dereference behavior changed â€” symlinks preserved instead of resolved â€” The old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync(srcFile)` which follows symlinks, so symlinked directories in `publicDir` had their contents recursively copied. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, preserving symlinks instead of resolving them. A user with a symlinked asset in `publicDir` will now get a dangling symlink in `dist/` instead of copied content. The call must include `dereference: true` to match prior behavior.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** â€” Same symlink regression in create-vite scaffolding copy â€” The removed `copy` function also used `fs.statSync(src)`, following symlinks. The replacement `fs.cpSync` call has the same `dereference: false` default, so any symlinked entry in a template directory would be scaffolded as a symlink rather than resolved content.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** â€” Self-copy guard silently removed â€” builds with nested outDir inside publicDir will now error â€” The old `copyDir` contained a guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that prevented infinite recursion when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`. While `cpSync` also detects this, it **throws** an error instead of silently skipping. A previously working build config (e.g., `dist` inside `public/`) will now fail hard with a runtime error.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** â€” Removed exported `copyDir` is a potential breaking change for downstream consumers â€” `copyDir` was an exported function from a public-facing utilities module. Removing it is a breaking change for any external or cross-package consumer that imports it (Vite plugins, ecosystem tooling, other internal modules). The PR updates one known consumer but does not verify all importers in the monorepo.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** â€” ExperimentalWarning at runtime on Node < 22.3 â€” The eslint-disable comment claims `fs.cpSync` is not experimental in Node 22.3+, which is true. However, Vite's supported Node range (18, 20, 22) means users on Node 18/20 will get an `ExperimentalWarning` at runtime in their CLI/build output. This is an observability concern for user-facing tooling.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** â€” ExperimentalWarning at runtime on Node < 22.3 (create-vite) â€” Same runtime concern as prepareOutDir.ts â€” users on Node 18/20 will see an `ExperimentalWarning` for `fs.cpSync` at runtime.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

Two critical symlink regression errors (prepareOutDir and create-vite), two warnings (self-copy guard removal and breaking API export removal), and two informational notes about ExperimentalWarnings on Node < 22.3. The reviewer recommends rejecting the PR unless `dereference: true` is added and the removed guard's edge case is addressed.

**6** finding(s): ðŸ”´ 2  Â·  ðŸŸ¡ 2  Â·  ðŸ”µ 2

- ðŸ”´ `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ â€” Symlink dereference behavior changed â€” symlinks preserved instead of resolved â€” The old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync(srcFile)` which follows symlinks, so symlinked directories in `publicDir` had their contents recursively copied. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, preserving symlinks instead of resolving them. A user with a symlinked asset in `publicDir` will now get a dangling symlink in `dist/` instead of copied content. The call must include `dereference: true` to match prior behavior.

  ```suggestion
  fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, dereference: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })
  ```
- ðŸ”´ `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ â€” Same symlink regression in create-vite scaffolding copy â€” The removed `copy` function also used `fs.statSync(src)`, following symlinks. The replacement `fs.cpSync` call has the same `dereference: false` default, so any symlinked entry in a template directory would be scaffolded as a symlink rather than resolved content.

  ```suggestion
  fs.cpSync(src, dest, { recursive: true, dereference: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })
  ```
- ðŸŸ¡ `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:627` _[Correctness, Security]_ â€” Self-copy guard silently removed â€” builds with nested outDir inside publicDir will now error â€” The old `copyDir` contained a guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that prevented infinite recursion when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`. While `cpSync` also detects this, it **throws** an error instead of silently skipping. A previously working build config (e.g., `dist` inside `public/`) will now fail hard with a runtime error.
- ðŸŸ¡ `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:624` _[Correctness, Security]_ â€” Removed exported `copyDir` is a potential breaking change for downstream consumers â€” `copyDir` was an exported function from a public-facing utilities module. Removing it is a breaking change for any external or cross-package consumer that imports it (Vite plugins, ecosystem tooling, other internal modules). The PR updates one known consumer but does not verify all importers in the monorepo.
- ðŸ”µ `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:88` _[Correctness, Security]_ â€” ExperimentalWarning at runtime on Node < 22.3 â€” The eslint-disable comment claims `fs.cpSync` is not experimental in Node 22.3+, which is true. However, Vite's supported Node range (18, 20, 22) means users on Node 18/20 will get an `ExperimentalWarning` at runtime in their CLI/build output. This is an observability concern for user-facing tooling.
- ðŸ”µ `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:646` _[Correctness, Security]_ â€” ExperimentalWarning at runtime on Node < 22.3 (create-vite) â€” Same runtime concern as prepareOutDir.ts â€” users on Node 18/20 will see an `ExperimentalWarning` for `fs.cpSync` at runtime.
